## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix (`fix:`)
- [ ] New feature / rule / skill / hook (`feat:`)
- [ ] Documentation only (`docs:`)
- [ ] Refactor — no behavior change (`refactor:`)
- [ ] Maintenance — gitignore, repo config (`chore:`)
- [ ] Other: <!-- specify -->

## Affects every fork?

<!-- Template / skeleton / mandatory rule changes propagate to everyone who clones this repo. -->

- [ ] Yes — this changes the template skeleton or a mandatory rule
- [ ] No — local addition only (new optional skill, new doc, bug fix)

## ADR

<!-- Structural changes (three-scope cascade, dual-primary, five-stage flow, skill curation policy, top-level folder changes) need an ADR. -->

- [ ] Not needed (local addition / bug fix / docs)
- [ ] ADR included in this PR (`decisions/ADR-NNNN-<slug>.md`)
- [ ] ADR landed separately and is `accepted`: ADR-NNNN

## Checklist

- [ ] I read [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] Commits follow Conventional Commits (`feat:` / `fix:` / `docs:` / ...)
- [ ] No unrelated reformatting / style changes
- [ ] Doc updates included if behavior changed
- [ ] Self-tested the change (e.g. `git clone` fresh + run the affected workflow)
